modules.description: Learn how laws are really made at the federal, state, and local level. This module walks you through each step a bill takes, from an idea to a signed law. You will see how regular people can shape this process at every stage.
learning_objectives: Identify the key stages a bill passes through at the federal level (introduction, committee, floor vote, conference, presidential action)
Describe how the Texas Legislature process differs from the federal process (140-day sessions, committee assignments by Speaker and Lt. Governor)
Explain how Houston city ordinances are proposed, heard, and adopted
Recognize at least three points where community members can influence the legislative process

videos.title: How a Bill Becomes a Law: Crash Course Government and Politics #9
videos.description: Fast-paced, entertaining breakdown of the federal legislative process with animations and humor

videos.title: Schoolhouse Rock: I'm Just a Bill
videos.description: The classic animated short that has taught generations how a bill becomes a law — a perfect warm-up

articles.title: Texas Legislature 101: How Bills Become Laws — and How You Can Participate in the Process
articles.description: Plain-language guide to the Texas legislative process with tips for citizen participation

song.title: A Change Is Gonna Come
song.why: A timeless anthem of hope and determination that reminds us meaningful change is always within reach

modules.hook_text: Every law that protects you, funds your child's school, or paves your street started as one person's idea written on a piece of paper.

modules.description: Bills can look confusing, but they follow a pattern. This module teaches you how to decode the language of legislation so you can understand what a bill actually does. Once you can read a bill, no one can mislead you about what it says.
learning_objectives: Identify the basic structure of a bill (title, caption, sections, definitions, effective date)
Distinguish between 'shall' (mandatory) and 'may' (optional) in legislative language
Read strikethrough and underline formatting to understand what a bill changes in existing law
Use Congress.gov and the Texas Legislature Online to look up and track bills

videos.title: Explore a Bill on Congress.gov
videos.description: Official walkthrough of how to navigate a bill's summary, text, actions, amendments, and cosponsors on Congress.gov

videos.title: The Legislative Process: Overview
videos.description: Brief overview video of the full legislative process with clear explanations of each stage

articles.title: Five Tips for Reading Legislation and Code
articles.description: Practical tips from attorneys on how to read bills without a law degree, including understanding hierarchy, cross-references, and key terms

song.title: Read All About It (Part III)
song.why: A song about finding your voice and speaking truth — because reading the bill is the first step to speaking up about it

modules.hook_text: A bill is just a story about how the world should work, written in a very specific language. Once you learn that language, you hold the power to shape the story.

modules.module_name: The Budget Is a Moral Document
modules.description: Every government budget tells a story about what a community values most. This module shows you how to follow the money in public budgets and understand who benefits. When you can read a budget, you can hold leaders accountable for their choices.
learning_objectives: Explain why budgets reflect community priorities and values
Navigate USAspending.gov and local budget documents to track public spending
Identify key budget categories at federal, state, and city levels
Describe how participatory budgeting empowers residents to direct public funds

videos.title: What If You Could Help Decide How the Government Spends Public Funds?
videos.description: Community organizer Shari Davis explains participatory budgeting and how everyday people can directly manage public money

videos.title: State Budget Basics Video Series
videos.source: National Association of State Budget Officers
videos.description: Short educational videos explaining how state budgets work, from revenue to appropriations

articles.title: A Citizens' Guide to Monitoring Government Expenditures
articles.description: Comprehensive guide empowering citizens to track and monitor how governments spend public money

song.title: Alright
song.why: An anthem of resilience and collective determination — because communities that understand their budgets can demand what they deserve

modules.hook_text: A budget is a moral document. Show me a budget and I'll show you who you think matters and who you think does not.
modules.hook_attribution: Adapted from Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and Senator Raphael Warnock

modules.description: Your voice matters in government meetings, and this module teaches you how to use it. Learn how to prepare and deliver powerful public testimony at city council, county meetings, and legislative hearings. Practice turning your experience into a message that decision-makers will remember.
learning_objectives: Describe the rules and process for speaking at Houston City Council (sign-up, time limits, format)
Structure a 2-minute testimony with a clear ask, personal story, and supporting evidence
Identify the difference between public comment, committee testimony, and written testimony
Practice delivering testimony confidently with timing and eye contact

videos.title: Public Comment 101: Effective Written and Spoken Testimonies
videos.description: Step-by-step guide with video on how to prepare and deliver effective public testimony

videos.title: How to Attend Houston City Council Meetings
videos.description: Houston-specific guide to attending and speaking at city council meetings, with practical tips

articles.title: Speak at City Council: Participate in City Council
articles.description: Houston-specific step-by-step guide for signing up to speak at council, including contact info (832.393.1100, speakers@houstontx.gov) and what to expect

song.title: Rise Up
song.why: A song about finding the courage to stand up and speak, even when it feels hard — just like walking up to that podium

modules.hook_text: The most powerful person in a government hearing isn't always the one behind the desk. Sometimes it's the person who stepped up to the microphone.

modules.description: No one changes the world alone. This module shows you how to build strong coalitions — groups of people and organizations working together toward a shared goal. Learn how to find allies, build trust, and create partnerships that last.
learning_objectives: Define what a coalition is and why coalitions are more powerful than individual advocacy
Identify potential coalition partners across sectors (faith, labor, business, neighborhood, nonprofit)
Apply the public narrative framework (Story of Self, Us, and Now) to build shared purpose
Describe strategies for managing disagreements and maintaining coalition unity

videos.title: The Power of Organizing — Betsy Hoover at TEDxDePaulU
videos.description: Obama campaign organizing director explains how to find, motivate, and unite people around a common cause

videos.title: Marshall Ganz: Public Narrative — Story of Self, Us, and Now
videos.source: Leading Change Network / Harvard Kennedy School
videos.description: Harvard professor Marshall Ganz teaches the storytelling framework used by organizers worldwide to build coalitions

articles.title: Coalition Building: Start Here
articles.description: Curated collection of guides, tools, and case studies for building effective coalitions for social change

song.title: Lean on Me
song.why: The ultimate anthem of mutual support — a reminder that coalitions are built on trust and showing up for each other

modules.hook_text: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.

modules.description: Great ideas need great communication to succeed. This module teaches you how to craft a message that moves people to action and how to use media — from social media to press conferences — to amplify your advocacy. Learn to tell stories that change minds.
learning_objectives: Apply the 'Story of Self, Us, and Now' framework to craft an advocacy message
Identify the elements of effective framing: values, villain, vision, and vehicle
Create a one-page messaging guide for a campaign or policy issue
Describe strategies for earned media (press releases, op-eds, social media)

videos.title: The Secret Structure of Great Talks — Nancy Duarte
videos.description: Presentation expert reveals the hidden structure behind history's most persuasive speeches, from MLK to Steve Jobs

videos.title: Public Narrative: How the Story of Self Becomes the Story of Us and Story of Now
videos.description: Learn the narrative framework that turns personal experience into a collective call to action

articles.title: Framing Equality Toolkit
articles.description: Practical toolkit for framing social change messages in ways that connect with people's values and inspire action

song.title: Glory
song.why: A song that demonstrates the power of storytelling in advocacy — connecting history, justice, and hope in a single anthem

modules.hook_text: The most powerful weapon on earth is the human soul on fire.

modules.description: Advocacy is a marathon, not a sprint. This final module helps you build habits that keep you strong for the long haul. Learn about self-care for activists, long-game strategy, and how to stay motivated when change feels slow.
learning_objectives: Recognize the signs of activist burnout and understand why it happens
Develop a personal sustainability plan that includes rest, boundaries, and community
Apply long-game thinking: setting milestones, celebrating small wins, and adjusting strategy
Identify at least three practices for sustaining energy and purpose over time

videos.title: How to Recover from Activism Burnout — Yana Buhrer Tavanier
videos.description: TED Fellow explores 'playtivism' — incorporating play and creativity into social change work to prevent burnout

videos.title: Using 5 Minutes of Self-Care to Survive Burnout — Racquel Armstrong
videos.description: A personal account of learning to put yourself first as a way of surviving burnout and continuing the work

articles.title: Self-Care for Activists: Sustaining Your Most Valuable Resource
articles.description: Conversation and resource collection on maintaining well-being while doing social change work

song.title: Three Little Birds
song.why: A gentle reminder that every morning brings new possibility — the perfect soundtrack for long-game thinking and staying grounded

modules.hook_text: Caring for myself is not self-indulgence. It is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare.

modules.description: Every neighborhood has strengths, stories, and people who make it special. This module helps you see your neighborhood through an asset-based lens — focusing on what you have, not what you lack. You will explore how history, culture, and identity shape the places we call home.
learning_objectives: Define asset-based community development (ABCD) and contrast it with deficit-based approaches
Identify at least five types of community assets (people, associations, institutions, physical spaces, economic resources)
Describe how neighborhood identity is shaped by history, culture, geography, and shared stories
Conduct a simple neighborhood asset inventory using observation and conversation

videos.title: Finding Identity in Community — Jay Grimes
videos.description: Explores how individual identity and community identity are deeply connected, and what makes a healthy community

videos.title: Creating a Connected Community — Dorice Ramsey
videos.description: Practical ideas for forging connections and creating a sense of belonging in your neighborhood

articles.title: Asset-Based Community Development: A Guide to Practices
articles.description: Comprehensive guide to the ABCD approach, including how to discover and mobilize existing community strengths

song.title: Home
song.why: A song about belonging and finding strength in the people around you — the heart of what makes a neighborhood

modules.hook_text: Every neighborhood already has everything it needs to thrive. The job of a leader is not to bring in what's missing — it's to discover what's already there.
modules.hook_attribution: Adapted from John McKnight and Jody Kretzmann, ABCD Institute

modules.description: Houston has a unique system of super neighborhoods, civic clubs, and HOAs that connects residents to city government. This module maps that civic infrastructure so you know exactly who to work with and how to plug in. Understanding these structures is your first step to neighborhood leadership.
learning_objectives: Explain the role and structure of Houston's 88 super neighborhoods and the Super Neighborhood Alliance
Describe the difference between civic clubs, HOAs, and management districts
Identify your own super neighborhood and its council leadership
Explain how super neighborhood action plans connect residents to city services and elected officials

videos.title: Exploring Super Neighborhoods: Their Role and Impact in Houston
videos.description: Houston-specific explainer of the super neighborhood system, how it connects residents to government, and why it matters

videos.title: What Is a Civic Club and Does Your Neighborhood Have One?
videos.description: Local news explainer on Houston civic clubs — what they do, how they work, and how to find yours

articles.title: City of Houston: Super Neighborhoods — About and Directory
articles.description: Official city resource with super neighborhood directory, alliance info, and how to participate in your local council

song.title: We're Not Gonna Take It
song.why: An energizing anthem about standing up together — because civic clubs are where neighbors organize to get things done

modules.hook_text: Houston is divided into 88 super neighborhoods. Each one has a direct line to city hall. Do you know which one is yours?

modules.description: Houston is the largest city in America with no zoning laws — and that makes it unlike anywhere else. This module explains how land use really works in Houston, from deed restrictions to minimum lot sizes. Understanding development is key to protecting and improving your neighborhood.
learning_objectives: Explain why Houston has no formal zoning and what 'de facto zoning' means in practice
Identify the tools Houston uses instead of zoning (deed restrictions, minimum lot sizes, parking requirements, historic districts)
Describe how development decisions affect neighborhoods (gentrification, displacement, mixed-use growth)
Recognize opportunities for residents to influence development through public processes

videos.title: Planning Without Zoning, Explained (Half as Interesting / Dave Amos)
videos.description: Explores how Houston regulates land use without a traditional zoning code, and what it means for residents

videos.title: Debunking Myths About Houston's Lack of Zoning (City Beautiful)
videos.description: Dives into common misconceptions about Houston's land use, revealing the hidden regulations that shape the city

articles.title: Houston Doesn't Have Zoning, But There Are Workarounds
articles.source: Rice University Kinder Institute for Urban Research
articles.description: In-depth article from local experts on how Houston's unique land use system actually works and its impact on neighborhoods

song.title: This Land Is Your Land
song.why: A song about who land belongs to — a fitting anthem for understanding land use and community ownership

modules.hook_text: Houston is the largest city in America without zoning. That means a nightclub can open next to a school — unless neighbors know how to use the tools they already have.

modules.description: When your street needs fixing, your park needs lights, or your block needs attention, there are real people and systems that can help. This module teaches you exactly how to navigate Houston's 311 system, connect with your council member, and work with county commissioners to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Use Houston 311 (call, app, and web) to submit and track service requests effectively
Identify your city council member, county commissioner precinct, and state representatives
Describe the roles and responsibilities of each level of local government in Houston/Harris County
Develop a strategy for escalating issues from 311 to elected officials when needed

videos.title: How to Attend Houston City Council Meetings
videos.description: Practical guide to showing up and participating in Houston city council meetings

videos.title: Your Guide to Houston 311
videos.description: Investigative look at how 311 service requests are handled, common misconceptions, and how to get results

articles.title: Houston 311: How to Use City Services
articles.description: Official guide to using Houston's 311 system by phone (dial 311 or 713-837-0311), web, and mobile app for service requests

song.title: Respect
song.why: A song about demanding what you deserve — because knowing how to work the system is how neighborhoods get the services they need

modules.hook_text: That pothole on your street? Someone's job to fix it. That broken streetlight? There's a number to call. The hardest part isn't getting things done — it's knowing who to ask.

modules.description: This module introduces the powerful tools communities use to build wealth and prevent displacement. Learn about community development financial institutions (CDFIs), community land trusts, and inclusive development strategies that keep neighborhoods affordable and thriving.
learning_objectives: Define CDFIs and explain how they provide financial services to underserved communities
Describe how community land trusts (CLTs) keep housing permanently affordable by separating land ownership from home ownership
Explain how the Houston Community Land Trust and Houston Land Bank work together
Identify strategies for inclusive development that prevents displacement while welcoming investment

videos.title: Faith, Hope and Capital: Community Development Financial Institutions
videos.description: Documentary video exploring how CDFIs and community land trusts create economic opportunity in underserved neighborhoods

videos.title: What Is a CDFI? Community Development Financial Institutions Explained
videos.description: Short explainer video on how CDFIs work and why they matter for community development

articles.title: Houston Community Land Trust: About Us
articles.description: Learn how the Houston CLT creates permanently affordable homeownership by separating land from housing through 99-year ground leases, with homes starting at $75,000

song.title: Beautiful Day
song.why: An uplifting song about seeing possibility even in tough circumstances — the spirit behind community development

modules.hook_text: What if your neighborhood could own the land under its own homes — forever? That's not a dream. It's called a community land trust, and Houston already has one.

modules.description: Everything you have learned comes together here. In this final module, you will create a real neighborhood action plan using the tools, relationships, and knowledge from the entire learning path. This is where learning becomes leadership.
learning_objectives: Conduct a neighborhood asset inventory (people, places, organizations, institutions, stories)
Identify three priority issues using community input and data
Map the civic infrastructure and decision-makers relevant to your priorities
Draft a neighborhood action plan with specific goals, timelines, responsible parties, and success measures

videos.title: The Power of Community — Dontae Latson
videos.description: Inspiring talk about the role every person can play in building community and how everyone's effort counts

videos.title: De'Amon Harges: The Roving Listener (Asset-Based Community Development in Action)
videos.description: A neighborhood organizer demonstrates how deep listening and discovering gifts transforms communities from the inside out

articles.title: The Community Visioning and Strategic Planning Handbook
articles.description: Step-by-step handbook for community visioning — from forming a steering committee to creating a shared neighborhood plan

song.title: We Are the Champions
song.why: A celebration of persistence and collective achievement — because finishing this path means you are ready to lead

modules.hook_text: You don't need permission to lead your neighborhood. You just need a plan, some neighbors, and the courage to start.
